channel.title: linux on sainnhe's blog
channel.description: Recent content in linux on sainnhe's blog

item.title: Blur Any Window in KDE Plasma
item.description: About The newest KDE plasma natively support blur, but it has many limitations, for example I can’t blur vim status line and firefox. This article tries to find a way to blur any window in plasma, like this: Preparation To begin with, make sure you have already enabled blur effect via Kvantum and some themes that support blur, for example Sweet Script I wrote a simple script that can launch application and blur the window automatically: plasma-blur.

item.title: 在 ArchLinux/SurfaceBook2 上安装配置 Nvidia 显卡驱动
item.description: 魔改内核 为了启用 SB2 的一些独有的特性如触摸屏和手写笔等，首先要把默认的内核换成魔改过后的内核，这里推荐 linux-surface/linux-

item.title: Sexy & Powerful Configuration for Lightline and Tmux
item.description: Nerd Font First of all, install a nerd font, and apply it: nerd font Then, pick a unicode over here: nerd font cheat sheet. In this case, search keyword sep After getting the unicode number, you can use it like this: :echo "\ue0b9" FAQ: Why are my nerdfont symbols not displaying correctly? The display of nerdfont can be affected by many factors, including terminal emulator, font type, font size, zoom, etc.
